title: Chrysanthemum eginense
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum eginense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae. It is a type of chrysanthemum, a genus of flowering plants native to East Asia. The specific classification of Chrysanthemum eginense is uncertain, but it is often placed in the section Leucanthemum. Further research is needed to determine its exact classification within the genus. The classification of Chrysanthemum eginense is still a topic of ongoing study and research in the field of botany.
uses: Chrysanthemum eginense, a species of flowering plant, has been traditionally used in folk medicine in China. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The plant is also used to relieve coughs, sore throats, and respiratory problems. In addition, Chrysanthemum eginense is believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and confirm its effectiveness.

intro: Chrysanthemum eginense, also known as Egin Chrysanthemum, is a type of flowering plant native to China, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating fever, and improving respiratory conditions. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum eginense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Chrysanthemum eginense is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments, including common cold and flu.
